shorts.script: Is Angi worth it? Here's the math nobody shows you. Angi sells the same lead to up to twelve contractors. So once you count the leads that don't close, your real cost per booked job runs about fourteen hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars. Compare that to roughly two-ninety for a job you win through your own site or referrals. Angi isn't a lead source. It's a tax on not having your own. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Is Angi Worth It for Contractors? The Real Cost Per Job #shorts
shorts.description:
The real cost of an Angi lead for contractors — why your leads get shared with up to 12 pros, and what it actually costs per booked job. Full breakdown + free calculator: https://booked-job.com/blog/is-angi-worth-it/

#shorts #contractor #angi #homeadvisor #thumbtack #trades #smallbusiness

shorts.script: Here's three ways your shop bleeds money you never even see. Number one: change orders you never billed for. The job grew, the invoice didn't. Number two: quick favors. There's no such thing as a five minute favor on a paying job. Number three: you're not charging for the drive. Windshield time is still time. Plug those three leaks, and you just gave yourself a raise. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: 3 Ways Your Contracting Business Bleeds Money #shorts
shorts.description:
Three money leaks every service business has — change orders, quick favors, and unbilled drive time. Fix them and give yourself a raise.

More for service pros: https://booked-job.com/

#shorts #contractor #smallbusiness #trades #pricing

shorts.hook: The cheapest bid costs the most
shorts.script: Ever lose a job to a guy half your price? Let him have it. He lowballs the bid to win it, then cuts corners to survive it. The customer pays twice. Once for the cheap job, once for you to fix it. Your price isn't high. It's honest. Don't chase the bottom. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Why the Cheapest Contractor Bid Costs the Most #shorts
shorts.description:
Stop apologizing for your price. The lowball bid always comes back around — and the customer pays twice.

https://booked-job.com/

#shorts #contractor #pricing #trades #smallbusiness

shorts.script: Here's the trap with lead sites. The day you stop paying, the leads stop. You rented an audience instead of building one. Every dollar into shared leads is a dollar not building something that keeps producing after you turn it off. Own your leads. Own your shop. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Stop Renting Your Leads (Angi, Thumbtack, HomeAdvisor) #shorts
shorts.description:
Shared-lead sites rent you an audience. The second you stop paying, the leads vanish. Build channels you own instead.

https://booked-job.com/blog/is-angi-worth-it/

#shorts #contractor #angi #leadgeneration #trades

shorts.hook: A cheap lead can be your most expensive one
shorts.script: Cost per lead is the bait. Cost per booked job is the meal. A cheap lead that never books is the most expensive lead there is. Stop judging a source by the sticker price. Judge it by what a booked job actually costs you. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Why Cheap Leads Are a Trap (CPL vs CPBJ) #shorts #contractor

shorts.hook: Customers find you through five doors
shorts.script: Customers come through five doors. Your Google profile, your website, paid ads, lead apps, and reviews. Two of them are free and they come first. If you're living behind just one door, you're one algorithm change from zero calls. Open more doors. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: The 5 Doors Every Customer Comes Through #shorts #trades

shorts.hook: Free real estate at the top of Google
shorts.script: The map pack is the top of local search. Three spots, totally free. Your Google Business Profile is what gets you in. Claim it, pick the right category, and add real photos of real work. Most contractors leave it half empty and wonder why they don't show up. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Win Google's Map Pack (It's Free) #shorts #contractor

shorts.hook: Your website has exactly one job
shorts.script: Your website has one job. Turn a visitor into a phone call. Most fail. The average contractor site converts two to three percent. Make it fast, make it mobile, put a giant tap to call number up top, show proof, and give one clear action. That's it. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Why 98% Leave Your Website #shorts #smallbusiness

shorts.hook: They judge you before you say a word
shorts.script: Before a customer ever hears your voice, they've judged you by your reviews. Count, recency, and how you respond all matter. A shop with a hundred and forty recent reviews beats one with twelve, even at a higher price. Reviews aren't vanity. They're your close rate. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Reviews Decide Who Gets the Call #shorts #trades

shorts.hook: A third of people now ask AI who to hire
shorts.script: Getting found used to mean Google. Now about a third of people ask an A I tool who to hire. Good news. The same foundation wins both. A strong profile, real reviews, and a clear website that actually answers questions. Build it once, get found everywhere. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Get Found by Google AND AI #shorts #contractor

shorts.hook: The five numbers that run your shop
shorts.script: Track five numbers every month. Cost per lead, cost per booked job, close rate, review count, and response time. Color each one green, yellow, or red. Knowing your numbers is the one thing that makes you impossible to fleece. Run it like an owner. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Your Marketing Scorecard: 5 Numbers #shorts #smallbiz

shorts.hook: A 50% markup is only a 33% margin
shorts.script: Most contractors don't lose money on the work. They lose it on the estimate. Here's the trap. A fifty percent markup is only a thirty three percent margin. Price off margin, not markup, or you'll work all year and wonder where it went. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Markup vs Margin: The Money Math #shorts #contractor

shorts.hook: The review system that actually works
shorts.script: Reviews decide who shows up in the map pack and who gets the call. Here's the system. Ask in person the second the job's done. Then text the direct review link within the hour. One tap for them. Ask every single customer. That's how twelve becomes a hundred. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: How to Actually Get Google Reviews #shorts #trades

shorts.hook: The slow season is won in the busy season
shorts.script: The slow season is won in the busy season. Bank a three to six month cushion while the work flows. Sell maintenance plans and weather proof jobs. Re engage past customers. And when everyone else cuts marketing, you increase it. That's how you eat all year. Follow Booked Job.
shorts.title: Survive the Slow Season #shorts #contractor
